examples.text: السَّرْدِينُ عِبَارَةٌ عَنْ سَمَكٍ مُدْهِنٍ صَغِيرٍ يُمْكِنُ أَكْلُهُ دُونَ مَضْغ.
examples.english: Sardines are small, oily fish that can be eaten whole.

etymology.text:
From the root س م ك (s m k), meaning “to be elevated or lofty”, “to be supported or held up”, “to sustain”; hence the meaning of “a source of sustenance”, “a mainstay or staple-food”. Cognate with Hebrew סָמַך (samak, “to lean, lay, rest, support”) attested in the Bible with the similar semantic sense “to sustain” or “to nourish”.

From the root س م ك (s m k) in the sense of "to nourish", "to make well-fed", "to fatten up livestock".

From the root س م ك (s m k), in the sense of "to be elevated or lofty", "to be supported or held up".
